claude_version.slot_note: Only 2 data-slot-id attributes across all 4 pages. Multiple content edits during testing may have stripped slots during page rebuilds.
claude_version.chakra_note: Sanskrit names not present in services.html. The content edits test (adding/removing services) likely caused a rebuild that dropped the chakra section detail.
accessibility_issues: Low slot count means most images lack alt text structure
strengths: Consistent header/footer across all pages via post-processing pipeline
Nav-sync and footer-sync across all pages
Dark theme colors match the Midnight Oracle palette from research
Content edits through chat interface (8/10 successful)
Booking form with Netlify form attributes
weaknesses: Only 2 image slots — most images hardcoded or missing
Content edits trigger full page rebuilds (120-180s each) instead of surgical replacements
Chakra detail lost during content editing rebuilds
Cross-page edits don't work (CTA change only applied to active page)
Every edit requires plan approval (adds 3 min overhead)

codex_version.slot_note: 17 data-slot-id attributes across all 4 pages with proper data-slot-role. Significantly better slot coverage than Claude build.
codex_version.chakra_note: All 7 Sanskrit names present (Muladhara, Svadhisthana, Manipura, Anahata, Vishuddha, Ajna, Sahasrara)
accessibility_issues: Needs audit — generated as standalone HTML without post-processing pipeline
strengths: Comprehensive content — 80% more HTML content than Claude build
All 7 chakras with Sanskrit names, colors, body locations
17 image slots with proper data-slot-id and data-slot-role
All 6 services with pricing on services page
Complete FAQ section on contact page
Proper Tailwind CSS configuration matching the Midnight Oracle palette
weaknesses: No post-processing pipeline (no nav-sync, footer-sync, CSS extraction)
No blueprint tracking or version history
No slot mapping persistence — images would need to be manually wired
Cannot be iteratively edited through Studio chat
Generated as a one-shot — no conversation context for refinement
Not integrated with Studio's deploy pipeline

verdict.reasoning: For a first-pass static site build, Codex produced more comprehensive, content-rich HTML with better slot compliance (17 vs 2), complete domain knowledge integration (all 7 Sanskrit chakra names), and 80% more content. The Claude build through Studio has the advantage of iterative editing, post-processing, and deployment integration, but the base HTML quality from Codex is significantly richer.
what_claude_did_better: Post-processing pipeline: nav-sync, footer-sync, CSS extraction, logo injection
Iterative editing: 8/10 content edits succeeded through chat
Deployment integration: one command to Netlify
what_codex_did_better: Content completeness: 94KB vs 52KB, more sections, more detail
Slot compliance: 17 slots vs 2 — properly structured for stock photo system
Domain knowledge: all 7 Sanskrit chakra names, detailed descriptions
One-shot quality: complete, well-structured pages without iteration
Pricing detail: all 6 services with correct pricing in services.html
verdict.recommendations_for_routing: Use Codex for initial page generation (richer HTML, better slot compliance, more content) and Claude/Studio for iterative refinement, content edits, and deployment. The ideal pipeline: Codex generates initial HTML → Studio imports it → Claude handles edits/refinement → Studio deploys. This combines Codex's generation strength with Studio's edit/deploy infrastructure.
